title: World History O/X
description: Key events and figures in history

cards.explanation: World War II ended in 1945 with the surrender of Germany and then Japan.

cards.prompt: The Great Wall is located in China.
cards.explanation: The Great Wall was built across northern China to defend against invasions.

cards.prompt: The first humans landed on the Moon in 1969.
cards.explanation: Apollo 11 carried the first humans to walk on the Moon in 1969.

cards.prompt: The ancient Egyptians built the pyramids at Giza.
cards.explanation: The pyramids at Giza were built by the ancient Egyptians as tombs for their pharaohs.

cards.prompt: The Roman Empire was centered on the Mediterranean Sea.
cards.explanation: At its height, the Roman Empire surrounded and controlled the Mediterranean region.

cards.prompt: Christopher Columbus reached the Americas in 1492.
cards.explanation: In 1492, Columbus crossed the Atlantic and reached islands in the Americas.
World War I began in 1914; 1950 was the start of the Korean War.

cards.prompt: The pyramids of Egypt were built by the ancient Romans.
cards.explanation: The pyramids were built by the ancient Egyptians, long before the Roman Empire existed.

cards.prompt: Napoleon was an emperor of England.
cards.explanation: Napoleon was an emperor of France, and he fought against Britain.

cards.prompt: The printing press with movable type in Europe was invented by Shakespeare.
cards.explanation: In Europe, Johannes Gutenberg is credited with the movable-type printing press; Shakespeare was a playwright.
The Berlin Wall fell in 1989. 1789 was the start of the French Revolution.

cards.prompt: Ancient Greece is famous for building the Taj Mahal.
cards.explanation: The Taj Mahal was built in India under the Mughal Empire, not in ancient Greece.
